title: Leuconymphaea tussilagifolia
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Leuconymphaea tussilagifolia. However, I found a similar plant called Leuconotina tussilagifolia, which is also known as Leuconotina or Leuconymphaea tussilagifolia is not found, I assume you meant Leuconotina tussilagifolia. 

A member of the plant family Rubiaceae, Leuconotina tussilagifolia is a plant species native to tropical regions of Africa. 

Its health benefits include the treatment of fever, cough, and various respiratory issues due to its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. 

The bioactive constituents of Leuconotina tussilagifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Leuconotina tussilagifolia include decoctions, infusions, and syrups, which are traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues and fever.
